hypothesis: If large language models are augmented with memory, periodic-table, and knowledge-base tools and used in an iterative propose–evaluate loop with diffusion-based structure generation and property prediction, then they will achieve higher success rates (within ±0.25 eV/atom of target formation energies), V.U.N. scores, and constraint adherence than tool-free LLMs or standalone generative models, because tool-guided planning and feedback-driven refinement more effectively navigate the composition–structure space.
